location: Unit 1 Closeburn Industrial Estate, Thornhill, DG3 5HR
why_them: Their 'website' is a bare WordPress.com blog with no phone number, no email, no quote form, no pricing, and no service area map — completely unfit for a business with 30+ years trading and a fuel supply operation on top of sweeping. They show up in NACS and Stovesonline directories, indicating legitimate commercial volume. A professional site would let them showcase both their sweep and firewood/fuel services in one place.
recommended_service: New professional website with online booking for chimney sweeps, fuel/firewood ordering page, service area map covering Thornhill, Closeburn, Moniaive, Sanquhar belt

why_them: Website returns an SSL handshake error on modern browsers, meaning most visitors will see a security warning and bounce — effectively invisible online despite 35 years in business. The domain is HTTP-only and the design is clearly pre-2015. They have a strong local reputation and Yell/Thomson Local listings that drive enquiries, but no modern presence to capture new customers.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild with SSL, online booking form, service area page, and certificate/insurance trust signals

location: Ken-Gart Cottage, Glenluce, Newton Stewart, DG8 0AA
why_them: Their website URL is 53 characters long and returns a 404, meaning they have effectively zero web presence for the whole of Wigtownshire. They are listed across Yell, Thomson Local, Kompass, and HotFrog confirming they are a real operating business with 10+ years experience, but any potential customer Googling 'drain unblocking Newton Stewart' or 'blocked drain Stranraer' will never find them. This is a pure new-build opportunity.
recommended_service: New website with clear service area (Wigtownshire focus — Newton Stewart, Stranraer, Wigtown, Whithorn), emergency call-out page, and contact/quote form

why_them: Rated #1 window cleaner in D&G for five consecutive years on ThreeBestRated, yet their entire online presence is a Facebook page — no website whatsoever. They offer gutters, fascias, soffits, solar panels and commercial cleaning alongside windows. A professional website would let them convert their word-of-mouth reputation into inbound leads rather than relying purely on directory referrals.
recommended_service: New multi-service website with quote request form, online booking, before/after gallery, and service area coverage map

why_them: Facebook-only presence for a cleaning business covering domestic, commercial, holiday home changeovers, deep cleans and laundry in Castle Douglas — a tourist-driven market where holiday let operators need reliable online booking and changeover scheduling. No website means no trust signals and no way to capture the holiday let management market that is growing rapidly in Galloway.
recommended_service: New website with holiday home changeover booking system, domestic clean quote form, and service area page for the Stewartry

why_them: Stranraer-based cleaning business covering a 30-mile radius including Newton Stewart, Wigtown, and Girvan. Website lwccleaning.co.uk returned a 403 Forbidden error, meaning customers clicking through from directory listings hit a dead page. They are listed on ReferenceLine and Bark, showing active customer demand, but the broken site is actively damaging their reputation.
recommended_service: New website to replace the broken/inaccessible existing one — service pages for domestic, commercial, window cleaning; quote form; service radius map

why_them: Operated by John Lynch with 40+ years of experience, but their basic website lacks a quote form, pricing transparency, and a service area page. The current site functions as a digital brochure but misses conversion opportunities — no online booking, no callout fee calculator, no emergency CTA.
recommended_service: Website refresh with emergency call-out landing page, service area page, pest-specific service pages with pricing, and online quote request form

location: Rostrum Buildings, Princes Street, Lochmaben, Lockerbie, DG11 1PQ
why_them: Professional WordPress site with decent design, but no online quote form, no service area page, and no emergency booking CTA — critical omissions for a 24/7 locksmith. Uses a personal Gmail address which undercuts trust. A locked-out customer at 11pm wants a quote form and an instant call button, not just a phone number.
recommended_service: Website upgrade: add service area map, emergency callout landing page with click-to-call, online quote form, and replace Gmail with branded email

why_them: Website uses a circa 2015 WordPress theme — dated design with no quote form and no service area page. They cover Castle Douglas and Dumfries with 30+ years of experience and around-the-clock emergency call-outs, yet the site does not reflect that professionalism. Castle Douglas has no other prominent local locksmith with a modern web presence.
recommended_service: Full website redesign: modern theme, emergency callout page, key-cutting service page, auto locksmith section, service area map, and online contact form

location: Saumarez, Serrick Road, Nethermill, Dumfries, DG1 3NQ
why_them: Offers window cleaning, gutter cleaning, pressure washing, and patio cleaning across Dumfries but has no website of their own, only a Thomson Local and ReferenceLine listing. With gutter/pressure washing services, customers routinely Google before booking, and the absence of any website means Klear View is invisible to that search traffic entirely.
recommended_service: New website covering all four services — window, gutter, pressure wash, patio — with per-service quote forms and a photo gallery

why_them: The genuine local independent skip hire company serving all of D&G but the website uses a self-signed SSL certificate that throws a browser security error, blocking most visitors. This is a direct conversion killer: customers trying to book online simply can't get through. The site also has no online booking or instant quote form.
recommended_service: Website rebuild with valid SSL, online skip booking/quote system, skip size guide, permit information for D&G, and a coverage area map
source: Google search, solway-plant-hire.co.uk SSL error confirmed

why_them: 30+ years of commercial cleaning with clients including Arnold Clark, BSW Timber, and NHS — strong pay capacity — but website last updated 2020, no online quote form, and dated layout. For a company bidding for commercial contracts, an outdated site can cost them tenders where procurement teams judge credibility by web presence.
recommended_service: Website refresh with case studies from named clients, tender-ready credentials page, clear commercial sector pages (retail, medical, offices), and a quote request form

why_them: Domain dngexteriorcleaning.com returns a 404 — the site is down or deleted. They are still indexed in Google and appear in pressure washing searches for Dumfries, meaning customers are clicking through to a dead URL. The business offers pressure washing, roof cleaning, moss removal, render cleaning, and gutter cleaning — five strong services with solid local demand.
recommended_service: New website to replace the dead domain, covering all five exterior cleaning services, with photo gallery, quote form, and D&G-focused service area page

why_them: 40+ operatives and ten years in business — a sizeable D&G cleaning operation. Their WordPress site is functional with a contact form and decent branding. The weakness is a generic Gmail contact address for a team of that size, limited SEO depth, and no online booking. At this scale they should be winning more commercial contracts with stronger digital authority.
recommended_service: Commercial cleaning landing pages by sector (care homes, offices, holiday lets), branded email setup, local SEO expansion pages, and online enquiry system upgrade
